_candidate_banner: CANDIDATE. Authored by AEOESS, offered for the kube-agentic-networking #298 stage family. Every verdict reproduces from this file alone under the closed stage_to_kind_to_verb table (self-checked 5/5). Candidate status pending the table decision on the equal-digest cross-stage case noted in the README.

_authored_against.bar: whichever side authors, the other reproduces every verdict from this file alone

_intent: Extend the #298 stage set with the negative directions it does not yet exercise: the reverse cross-stage object case, stage-kind rejects landing on the agent-declared object stage rather than controller-observed-object, and the unknown_stage verdict that the enum defines but no vector exercises.

stage_model.note: Reference copy of the closed table from #298. The kind is fixed per stage; the verb derives from the stage. Object stages verify by recompute, the effect stage by compare.

stage_verdicts.bound: object stage; the commitment digest equals the recompute at the named stage
stage_verdicts.digest_mismatch: object kind, same stage; the recompute diverges (tampering)
stage_verdicts.cross_stage_digest_mismatch: object kind, commitment stage differs from the recompute stage; a digest mismatch caused by the pipeline (e.g. defaulting), not tampering
stage_verdicts.stage_kind_mismatch: the required stage and the commitment stage are different kinds; a category error rejected before any recompute or compare
stage_verdicts.unknown_stage: the named stage is not in the closed enum
stage_verdicts.confirmed: effect stage; an independent observation matches the declaration
stage_verdicts.contradicted: effect stage; an independent observation diverges from the declaration
stage_verdicts.unobserved: effect stage; no independent observation, so the receipt stays a declaration

vectors._asserts: Reverse of st3. The commitment was taken at controller-observed-object (defaulted object) but the required stage is agent-declared. Object kind on both sides, stages differ, so the divergence is the pipeline, not tamper.

Effect commitment required at the agent-declared object stage. st5 used controller-observed-object as the object side; this exercises the other object stage. Different kinds, rejected before any verb.

Object commitment at agent-declared required at the effect stage. st6 used a controller-observed-object commitment; this exercises the agent-declared object stage. Different kinds, rejected before any verb.
The required stage names a value outside the closed enum. The #298 set defines unknown_stage but no vector exercises it. Adjacent to the cross-stage and stage-kind ask, included for enum coverage.
The commitment names a stage outside the closed enum. Covers the unknown_stage path from the commitment side as well as the required side.
